title: Melancranis zeyheri
taxonomy: Melancranis zeyheri is classified within the family Apocynaceae. The genus Melancranis is part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. This classification suggests that Melancranis zeyheri is related to other plants in the same family, such as the milkweed and oleander plants. Further research into the specific taxonomy of Melancranis zeyheri may reveal more detailed information. The classification is based on characteristics such as leaf structure and flower morphology.
constituents: Melancranis zeyheri, also known as the "wild guelder rose", has been found to contain various active constituents, including iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. These compounds have been reported to have potential therapeutic properties, such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's iridoid glycosides, specifically, may contribute to its traditional uses in treating various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of these active constituents on human health. Further studies are necessary to isolate and characterize the specific active constituents present in Melancranis zeyheri.
